zh.Englefield, Berkshire, England: 英國波克夏恩格利費爾德
zh.lifeSummary: Jane De Milborne於1433年出生。她於1455年之前與John D'englefield在艾歐洛結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1498年享年65歲。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Milborne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Milburn
meanings: English (Northumberland, Cumberland and Durham): habitational name from any of the places in Westmorland, Northumberland, Somerset, Dorset or Wiltshire, all derived from Old English myln ‘mill’ + burna ‘spring, stream’. There has been some confusion with Melbourne .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: Jane De Milborne was born in 1433. She married John D'englefield before 1455, in Europe. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1498, at the age of 65.

fr.lifeSummary: Jane De Milborne est née en 1433. Elle a épousé John D'englefield avant 1455, à Europe. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée en 1498, à l’âge de 65 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Jane De Milborne wurde 1433 geboren. Sie hat vor 1455 in Europa John D'englefield geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1498 im Alter von 65 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Jane De Milborne è nata il nel 1433. Lei sposato John D'englefield prima del 1455, nel Europa. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1498, all'età di 65 anni.

ja.Englefield, Berkshire, England: イギリスバークシュアイアエングレフィールド
ja.lifeSummary: Jane De Milborneは1433年に生まれた。1455年以前にヨーロッパでJohn D'englefieldと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1498年に65歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane De Milborne(은)는 1433년 태어났다. 그녀는 1455년 이전 우럽에서 John D'englefield(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1498년 65세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Jane De Milborne nasceu em 1433. Ela casou-se com John D'englefield antes 1455, em Europa. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 1498, com 65 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Jane De Milborne родилась в 1433 г. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John D'englefield перед 1455 г. в местности Европа. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1498 г. в возрасте 65 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Jane De Milborne nació en 1433. Se casó con John D'englefield antes de 1455, en Europa. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1498, a la edad de 65 años.
